content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance/Administration . . . Executive/Legal . . . Enforcement/Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5660                                    322-5901                    322-6441

July 14, 1976


Mr. Robert P. Will
General Counsel
Metropolitan Water District of
Southern California
1111 Sunset Boulevard
P. O. Box 54153
Los Angeles, CA 90054

Re: Correction of Representations Regarding Enforcement of
    Government Code Section 87207(b)

Dear Mr. Will:

     In responding by letter of June 9 to your request for an
extension of the deadline for the submission of the Conflict
of Interest Code for the Metropolitan Water District, I
made the following representation:

     ...The Commission is cognizent of the pervasive
     uncertainty caused by the issues raised in Hays v.
     Wood. Accordingly, with respect to public officials
     who must disclose pursuant to 87207(b), the Commission
     will take no enforcement action against persons who
     fail to file the names of clients pursuant to 87207(b)
     of the Political Reform Act on the basis of Hays v.
     Wood.

     On July 7, 1976, the Commission met in Executive Session
to discuss this representation. At that time, the Commission
determined that the representation was erroneous, and that the
following is a correct interpretation of current Commission policy
on this subject.

     The Commission will continue to enforce the law as written
until such time as the law is stayed by a court order, ruled
invalid by a court or changed by the Legislature. Accordingly,
the Commission shall inform persons who decline to comply with
Section 87207(b) that they are required to do so and that
noncompliance places them in violation of the Political Reform
Act.


- 2 -

As a practical matter, the Commission regards Hays v. Wood
as a test case with respect to the constitutality of
Section 87207(b)(2) and, therefore, has not, to this date,
initiated any other judicial or administrative enforcement
proceedings pending the outcome of that lawsuit. However, this
practice should not be considered tantamount to a policy of nonenforcement. The Commission still expects public officials with
disclosure obligations under the Act to comply fully. Further,
the Commission reserves the right to initiate other litigation or
administrative enforcement proceedings should it be deemed
appropriate in a particular situation.

Accordingly, the same policy will be in effect for the
enforcement of provisions comparable to Government Code §87207(b)
found in Conflict of Interest Codes, for which the Commission is
the code reviewing body.

Because of certain probable changes in the Commission's
regulations defining terms within Government Code §§87100 and 87103
and the applicability of such terms to Conflict of Interest Codes,
the hearing dates of the Conflict of Interest Codes before the
Commission and the submission dates to the Commission for Conflict
of Interest Codes have been suspended.

Please feel free to raise any issues related to the effectuation of the Commission's stated policy through Conflict of Interest
Codes when your Code is rescheduled for hearing before the Commission.

Thank you.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr., Chief
Conflicts of Interest Division

DLS:mra


The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California

Board of General Counsel                                May 21, 1976

CERTIFIED MAIL
RETURN RECEIPT REQUESTED

Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
Chief, Conflict of Interest Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California  95814

     Request for Extension of Time to
     File Conflict of Interest Code

Dear Mr. Spurlock:

     The Metropolitan Water District of Southern California has been directed to submit a conflict of interest code to the Fair Political Practices Commission on or before June 10, 1976. Facts that have arisen during the formulation and review of Metropolitan's proposed code indicate, however, that an extension of time for filing is necessary.

     The request for extension arises out of a provision in Metropolitan's proposed code that requires members of the Board of Directors, who are also attorneys, to disclose the names of certain clients. The provision is identical to Section 87207(b) of the Political Reform Act of 1974, except that it is modified by the foreseeability and materiality requirements of Section 87302(b) of the Act.

     It is strongly believed by members of Metropolitan's Board that the client disclosure requirement is unconstitutional and in conflict with the professional responsibilities of attorneys. After a review of the legal issues involved, I must concur. The reasons are well articulated in the amicus curiae brief of the State Bar which has been submitted to the Superior Court for the County of Mendocino in the case of Hays v. Wood (No. 36319). A copy of the brief is therefore attached in support of this request.

     You are no doubt already familiar with Hays v. Wood and realize the import of the forthcoming decision on Metropolitan's directors. Since the case has been set for trial

The Metropolitan Water District, Los Angeles, Calif.  Mailing address: Box 54153, Los Angeles, Calif. 90054 / Telephone: (213) 626-4252


Mr. Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.        -2-                May 21, 1976


on May 27, 1976, I therefore request, on behalf of Metro-
politan, a waiver of the June 10 deadline. We would then
be prepared to file Metropolitan's conflict of interest code
within 15 days after a decision is rendered by the trial
court in Hays v. Wood. Accordingly, Metropolitan's code
would reflect the court's ruling on disclosure of client
names by attorneys serving on Metropolitan's Board of Directors.

                                          Very truly yours,


                                          [signature]
                                          Robert P. Will
                                          General Counsel


Enclosure


TO:         CENTRAL FILE

THRU:       J. PRITCHARD

FROM:       [illegible]

RE:         Code §§ and Regulations Referenced:
            [illegible]                    §2230
                                          §7207(D)


                                          May 12, 1976


            Robert Booth
            City Attorney
            Civic Center
            250 Hamilton Avenue
            Palo Alto CA  94301

            Dear Mr. Booth:

                  Attached please find a letter which was sent to
            Mr. Scott Carey of the real estate brokerage firm of
            Cornish and Carey. Mr. Carey called me and indicated
            that he was the councilman about whom you and I had talked.
            He also stated that although he receives more than
            10% of the profits of the firm, he has no ownership
            interest in the firm.  It was in light of this information
            that I advised him that he does not have a disclosure
            obligation at this time.

                  I advised Mr. Carey that I would inform you of this
            advice. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate
            to contact me.

                                          Very truly yours,

                                          [signature]
                                          Kenneth Finney
                                          Chief,
                                          Legal Division

            KF:pvp
            Enclosure

sections.conclusion: The Commission will continue to enforce the law as written until such time as the law is stayed by a court order, ruled invalid by a court or changed by the Legislature. The Commission will inform persons who decline to comply with Section 87207(b) that they are required to do so and that noncompliance places them in violation of the Political Reform Act. However, the Commission has not to date initiated other judicial or administrative enforcement proceedings pending the outcome of Hays v. Wood as a test case.
sections.question_synthetic: The document addresses a correction to prior FPPC representation regarding enforcement of Government Code Section 87207(b) requiring disclosure of client names by attorneys in positions with disclosure obligations, in the context of the pending Hays v. Wood constitutional challenge.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC corrected its prior representation, stating it will enforce Section 87207(b) as written while treating Hays v. Wood as a test case on constitutionality, and has suspended enforcement proceedings pending that lawsuit's outcome. The same policy applies to comparable provisions in Conflict of Interest Codes.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains multiple letter segments from different dates (July 14, 1976; May 21, 1976; May 12, 1976). Primary FPPC response letter is clear, but document compilation includes incoming requests and internal correspondence. The main document is a correction letter from the Conflicts of Interest Division Chief to the Metropolitan Water District's General Counsel.

CONCLUSION: The Commission will continue to enforce the law as written until such time as the law is stayed by a court order, ruled invalid by a court or changed by the Legislature. The Commission will inform persons who decline to comply with Section 87207(b) that they are required to do so and that noncompliance places them in violation of the Political Reform Act. However, the Commission has not to date initiated other judicial or administrative enforcement proceedings pending the outcome of Hays v. Wood as a test case.

embedding.summary: This document corrects a prior FPPC representation regarding enforcement of attorney client disclosure requirements under Government Code Section 87207(b), clarifying that the Commission will continue enforcing the law while treating the pending Hays v. Wood case as a constitutional test case. The document also references correspondence regarding an extension request from the Metropolitan Water District and a disclosure determination for a Palo Alto councilman.
